serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of Ginger & Onion Beef, often served as a main dish with rice, is approximately 250 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Beef, stir-fried with vegetables
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient data adapted from a generic 'Beef, stir-fried with vegetables' entry due to lack of a specific 'Ginger & Onion Beef' entry in approved sources. Values are representative of this dish category.

gi_source.title: Estimation based on similar low-carbohydrate stir-fries
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index estimated as low (35) due to the dish's primary composition of lean beef and non-starchy vegetables, resulting in a low carbohydrate content. Analogous to other meat and vegetable stir-fries without significant added sugars or starches, which typically have low GI values per the University of Sydney GI Database.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume this dish in moderation due to its sodium content (~318 mg per 100 g), primarily from soy sauce and other seasonings; opting for reduced-sodium soy sauce or preparing at home with less salt can help manage intake.

type2_diabetes.guidance: Ginger & Onion Beef is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low carbohydrate (~2.8 g per 100 g) and sugar content, contributing to a low glycemic load of approximately 2.45 per serving; portion control is still advised, especially if served with rice.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While a good source of protein, individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of the saturated fat content (~2.1 g per 100 g) from beef and cooking oil; choosing leaner cuts of beef and minimizing added oil can help reduce cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ginger & Onion Beef (姜葱牛肉) is a classic Chinese stir-fry dish featuring tender slices of beef wok-fried with aromatic fresh ginger and crisp scallions (spring onions). The beef is typically marinated in soy sauce, rice wine, and cornstarch to enhance flavour and tenderness, then quickly stir-fried over high heat with thinly sliced ginger and chopped scallions in a savoury sauce often incorporating oyster sauce, light soy sauce, and a touch of sesame oil. The dish is characterised by its robust umami flavour, distinct warming ginger aroma, and the slight crunch of the onions. It is commonly served hot as part of a multi-dish Chinese meal alongside steamed white rice. Nutritionally, it is a good source of protein (~12.8 g per 100 g) and provides essential micronutrients including iron, zinc, and B vitamins from the beef, while being relatively low in carbohydrates (~2.8 g per 100 g), resulting in a low glycemic load. Sodium content is moderate due to soy-based sauces, and saturated fat should be considered by those managing cardiovascular health.

confidence_reason: Nutrient data adapted from a similar USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for beef stir-fry with vegetables; no exact match for 'Ginger & Onion Beef' exists in approved primary sources. Glycemic index is an analogous estimate based on the low carbohydrate profile of the dish and similar low-GI meat and vegetable preparations from the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g is set to -1 as this value is not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy source entry for beef stir-fried with vegetables. polyunsaturated_fat_g is set to -1 as this value is not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy source entry for beef stir-fried with vegetables. trans_fat_g is set to -1 as this value is not reported in the adapted USDA SR Legacy source entry for beef stir-fried with vegetables. All other -1 sentinel values (potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g) have been replaced with numeric estimates from the adapted USDA source. The dish is served at Riverfront Seafood - Boat Quay on field survey data at SGD 19.80.
